zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Alyce Atkin于1527年12月出生在英国奧可福爾时，她的父亲John Atkin12岁，母亲Lady Agnes Chrichton17岁。她于1540年与Tolderbh Tolderbury在英国结婚。她去世于1596年7月8日在英国英格兰奧可福爾奧可福德享年68岁。

nameMeanings.name: Atkin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Atkin (composed of Ade + the diminutive suffix -kin). This is one of the many medieval pet forms of Adam . Compare Scottish Aitken .

variants.name: Adam
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Vito, Angelo, Antonio, Salvatore, Giuseppe, Santo, Giovanna, Nicola, Aldo, Baldassare, Carmelo, Carmino.
Italian: from the personal name Adamo, the Italian form of Adam .

variants.name: Akin
meanings: English: variant of   Atkin   or   Aitken  .
Turkish (Akın): ornamental name, nickname or metonymic occupational name from the military term akın ‘(commando-) raid, foray’.

variants.name: Aikens
meanings: English: variant of Atkin , with genitival or excrescent -s (compare Atkins ).

Dutch and East Frisian: patronymic from the personal name Aike, a diminutive of Aai/Aie, a variant or a short form of the personal names Aaielt, Age, Ad(d)e or Adriaan. In North America, this surname may also be an altered form of the variant Eikens .

variants.name: Atkinson
meanings: English (northern): patronymic from the personal name Atkin or Adkin, pet forms of Adam.

variants.name: Atkins
meanings: English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name Atkin (a pet form of Adam) with genitival -s, or a variant of Atkin , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

nameMeanings.name: Alyce
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Alyce Atkin was born in December 1527, in Oxfordshire, England, her father, John Atkin, was 12 and her mother, Lady Agnes Chrichton, was 17. She married Tolderbh Tolderbury in 1540, in England. She died on 8 July 1596, in Oxford, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 68.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Alyce Atkin est née en décembre 1527, à Oxfordshire, Angleterre, son père, John Atkin, avait 12 ans et sa mère, Lady Agnes Chrichton, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé Tolderbh Tolderbury en 1540, à Angleterre. Elle est décédée le 8 juillet 1596, à Oxford, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 68 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Alyce Atkin im Dezember 1527 in Oxfordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Atkin 12 und ihre Mutter Lady Agnes Chrichton 17. Sie hat 1540 in England Tolderbh Tolderbury geheiratet. Sie starb am 8. Juli 1596 im Alter von 68 Jahren in Oxford, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Alyce Atkin è nata il nel dicembre 1527 nel Oxfordshire, England, sua padre, John Atkin, aveva 12 anni e sua madre, Lady Agnes Chrichton, aveva 17 anni. Lei sposato Tolderbh Tolderbury nel 1540, nel England. Morì il 8 luglio 1596, nel Oxford, Oxfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 68 anni.
